title: Self-host — run fab install + fab doctor --fix to refresh three-end blocks with new cite policy section
scope: repo bootstrap files (.claude/AGENTS.md, .codex/AGENTS.md, .cursor/CLAUDE.md or equivalent paths) + .fabric/events.jsonl
description: Eat our own dog food: after TASK-01..09 land, the local repo's three-end managed blocks still hold the rc.19 canonical (without cite policy). Run `fab install --reinstall` (or whatever the current re-sync invocation is) followed by `fab doctor --fix` to detect drift and rewrite the managed blocks with rc.20 BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL (now containing `## Cite policy`). Verify by `fab doctor --strict` exits 0. Also smoke-test the cite-coverage path: run `fab doctor --cite-coverage` once locally to emit the marker + confirm the new flow works end-to-end on the live repo.

files.target: managed block between BOOTSTRAP_MARKER_BEGIN and BOOTSTRAP_MARKER_END
files.change: Rewrite via `fab install` to contain new BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL including `## Cite policy` H2 section (locked verbatim text from TASK-01).

files.path: .codex/AGENTS.md (or wherever Codex managed block lives in this repo)
files.change: Refreshed by `fab install` to mirror Claude Code.

files.path: .cursor/CLAUDE.md (or wherever Cursor managed block lives — verify before run)

files.change: Single new line appended: cite_policy_activated event (emitted by first `fab doctor --cite-coverage` invocation).

implementation: Verify TASK-01..09 are committed and the workspace builds clean (`pnpm -r build`).
Snapshot current three-end block contents (`git diff` baseline).
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli build` to rebuild CLI with rc.20 changes.
Run `pnpm exec fab install --reinstall` (or current rc.20 install command) in repo root.
Run `pnpm exec fab doctor --strict` — expect drift detected on first run since canonical changed; auto-fix flow may need explicit `fab doctor --fix`.
Run `pnpm exec fab doctor --fix` to rewrite managed blocks.
Run `pnpm exec fab doctor --strict` again — expect exit 0 (clean).
Run `pnpm exec fab doctor --cite-coverage` — expect zero-metric report + marker_emitted_now warning + single cite_policy_activated event appended to .fabric/events.jsonl.
Verify .claude/AGENTS.md (and other end blocks) now contain `## Cite policy` H2 — `grep -l '## Cite policy' .claude/AGENTS.md .codex/AGENTS.md .cursor/CLAUDE.md` (or whatever the actual paths are).
Commit the refreshed files. NOTE: do NOT commit any one-off probe events to .fabric/events.jsonl unless that's the established convention; check `git diff .fabric/events.jsonl` and decide.

reference.pattern: Self-host bootstrap refresh after canonical bump
reference.examples: Recent rc.19 commits (bfecffa, ed4bc56) bumped canonical + refreshed end blocks via same flow.

rationale.chosen_approach: Run install + doctor --fix locally to refresh end blocks — same procedure as rc.19 self-host commit. Smoke-test the new --cite-coverage path on the live repo to catch any integration bugs that unit tests missed.
alternatives_considered: Skip self-host this RC, defer to rc.21 (rejected: leaves repo in inconsistent state where local AGENTS.md doesn't reflect canonical)
Manual edit of three-end blocks (rejected: bypasses the managed-block writer that real users depend on; can introduce drift)
decision_factors: Eats own dog food — catches install-path bugs
Establishes the canonical snapshot for downstream RCs to drift-detect against
rationale.tradeoffs: First --cite-coverage run will emit a marker event to the repo's .fabric/events.jsonl — need to decide whether to commit that. Probably YES to give downstream RCs a baseline marker, but verify against rc.17 / rc.19 events.jsonl commit convention.

manual_checks: `fab doctor --cite-coverage --json` returns valid JSON with status:'ok' and marker_emitted_now:true on first run, false on second
Visually inspect the rewritten managed blocks for correctness (no mangled markdown, no escape issues)
success_metrics: Three end-block files all contain `## Cite policy` section
cite_policy_activated event present in .fabric/events.jsonl with valid Zod shape
`fab doctor --cite-coverage` runs without error on the live repo
No spurious files modified beyond the expected three end blocks + events.jsonl
integration: Self-host smoke: full fab install + fab doctor --fix + fab doctor --strict + fab doctor --cite-coverage chain

criteria: All three end-block files (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor) contain `## Cite policy` H2
`fab doctor --cite-coverage --json` returns status:'ok' (or status:'skipped' only on absolute first run before marker emit)
Git diff shows only the expected file changes (3 end-blocks + 1 events.jsonl line; no spurious diffs)
Visual inspection: rewritten managed blocks render correctly (markdown syntax intact, no escape issues)
Commit msg: `chore(rc20): self-host — refresh three-end blocks with cite policy (TASK-10)`
